text:
armas
SARAVALI.
Chapter 40.
Calculation of Longevity
1. The people all over remain perplexed over the question of longevity. So I narrate the relative
principles based on the Shastras here under.
2-3. SELECTION OF SYSTEM FOR LONGEVITY COMPUTATION. Out of the triothe Sun, the
Moon and the Ascendant, if the Ascendant is the strongest, Amsayurdaya should be resorted
to. Pindayu is the best, if the Sun is predominant, while Nisargayu is the apt choice with
Moon prevailing. If the three in question are bereft of strength, then Jeevasarmas system can
be used.
4. AMSAYURDAYA CALCULATIONS.
The longitudes (counted from Aries first point) of the (seven) planets and the Ascendant should
be divided by 200 to get the number of years. The quotients in later processes should be
multiplied by the respective multipliers to get months, days and Ghatis.
5. ADDITIONS FOR ASCENDANTS CONTRIBUTION.
If the Ascendant is strong the number of years equal to the Rasis it has traversed should be
added. Months etc.
